info.title: Image Studio
info.text: A compact guide to the studio: create images, edit with attached images, run batches, inspect results and keep provider settings under control.
info.quickText: The main flow stays short: prompt, attachments, controls and the result archive.
info.quick.model.title: Model first
info.quick.model.text: Choose the active model in Settings or from the composer controls before sending real work.
info.quick.images.title: Images are flat attachments
info.quick.images.text: Attach the images the model should consider; use the prompt to say which one must be edited. Masks remain a separate edit control.
info.quick.trace.title: Details keep the trace
info.quick.trace.text: Every finished request stores prompt, files, parameters, status and technical payload for later inspection.
info.guidesText: Short routes for common actions: composer controls, image wall, result details and batch queue.
info.guide.generate.title: Regular generation
info.guide.generate.text: Use this when you need a new result from text only.
info.guide.generate.step1: Open composer controls and keep the mode set to Generate.
info.guide.generate.step2: Write the prompt; open Parameters only when you need size, quality, background, output or retry controls.
info.guide.generate.step3: Send the request; the result appears in the gallery and is available on the detail page.
info.guide.edit.title: Image editing
info.guide.edit.text: Use this when images should guide or become part of the edit.
info.guide.edit.step1: Switch the composer to Edit and add one or more images above the prompt.
info.guide.edit.step2: Describe in the prompt what image should change and what should stay the same.
info.guide.edit.step3: Use Add mask only when the provider supports a PNG mask and the edit must be limited to an area.
info.guide.batch.title: Batch generation
info.guide.batch.text: A queue of independent requests with their own prompts, attachments and parameters.
info.guide.batch.step1: Open Batch generation from composer controls and add the needed requests.
info.guide.batch.step2: Select a queue request and edit its prompt, attachments, model, mask and parameters from controls.
info.guide.batch.step3: Set the interval between sends, then run the queue; results are collected in one batch item.
info.guide.details.title: Details and reruns
info.guide.details.text: The detail page shows the image and the request data.
info.guide.details.step1: Open a result from the gallery to inspect the image without losing its request context.
info.guide.details.step2: Use the inspector for prompt, files, sent parameters, status and technical payload.
info.guide.details.step3: Copy prompt/payload/parameters or load the request back into the composer for another iteration.
info.guide.providers.title: Providers and models
info.guide.providers.text: A provider stores endpoints and auth; a model stores the technical model ID and provider link.
info.guide.providers.step1: In Settings, add or select a provider and fill generation/edit endpoints, key, headers and timeout.
info.guide.providers.step2: Add models, set their model IDs and attach them to the right provider.
info.guide.providers.step3: Save settings and choose the active model; it is available in composer controls.
info.guide.probe.title: API checks
info.guide.probe.text: Checks help discover what parameters a provider accepts before real work starts.
info.guide.probe.step1: Run Quick check first to verify that the selected provider route responds.
info.guide.probe.step2: Run the full capability probe when you want the app to hide unsupported parameters automatically.
info.guide.probe.step3: After probing, the capability profile is cached locally and reused by the parameter UI.
